records.ety: [F. procder. fr. L. procedere, processum, to go before, to proceed; pro forward + cedere to move. See Cede.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Proceeded; p. pr. & vb. n. Proceeding.]

defs: To move, pass, or go forward or onward; to advance; to continue or renew motion begun; as, to proceed on a journey.
quotes: If thou proceed in this thy insolence.

defs: To pass from one point, topic, or stage, to another; as, to proceed with a story or argument.
To issue or come forth as from a source or origin; to come from; as, light proceeds from the sun.
quotes: I proceeded forth and came from God.
It proceeds from policy, not love.

defs: To go on in an orderly or regulated manner; to begin and carry on a series of acts or measures; to act by method; to prosecute a design.
quotes: He that proceeds upon other principles in his inquiry.

defs: To be transacted; to take place; to occur.
quotes: He will, after his sour fashion, tell you What hath proceeded worthy note to-day.

defs: To have application or effect; to operate.
quotes: This rule only proceeds and takes place when a person can not of common law condemn another by his sentence.

defs: To begin and carry on a legal process.
